food.name: Bacon

identity.basisReason: Raw per-100g as-sold basis matches package-label style nutrition and avoids cooked-water-loss distortion.
includes: plain cured sliced bacon as sold

scoreReadiness.summary: Core raw bacon nutrition is coherent enough for launch scripting, but a few protein-quality style fields are still older calibration proxies rather than locked source facts.
blockers: collagen and protein-quality proxy metrics still need either exact-source backing or deliberate removal
cured bacon product variability means exact sodium-forward real-world burden is broader than this simplified nutrition record captures

sourceNotes: Identity locked to cured pork bacon, unprepared, per 100 g as sold.
Core nutrient values align with USDA SR Legacy style pork bacon cured unprepared entries.
Raw basis kept intentionally, not cooked bacon values.

topPros.title: highly palatable and appealing
topPros.explanation: A real-world reason people love it.

topPros.title: works as a strong flavour booster
topPros.explanation: Useful in meals even in smaller amounts.

topPros.title: has strong comfort-food appeal
topPros.explanation: Popular and instantly legible for content packaging.

topCons.title: sodium is a major drawback
topCons.explanation: A central practical health issue for bacon.

topCons.title: processing is a major negative
topCons.explanation: A key downside now that more processed foods are in scope.

topCons.title: rarely improves a meal much on its own
topCons.explanation: Usually works more as a flavour add-on than a genuinely strong meat choice.

explanation.summary: Pros are carrying this food most, while cons are holding it back most. It lands in D tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in D tier with an overall score of 22.4. Section scores: fats 48.9, proteins 20, vitamins 12, minerals 3.8, pros 50, cons 0.

reviewChecklist: Verify nutrient values are sourced well enough for publishable use.
Review narration for category repetition and trim any awkward lines.
Confirm subtitle density stays readable at phone speed.
Replace placeholder hero/sprite art with final asset selection.
Check that the final tier verdict feels editorially defensible.
nextHumanStep: Review the generated narration + manifest, then use the manifest as the handoff into visual assembly / editing.
